source_notes.exports: Kpler public data — Novorossiysk weekly crude+product volumes

strike_weights.notes: Weighted score capped at 100. Rolling 30-day window.

export_baseline.description: Pre-war annual average Novorossiysk crude+product export volume used as 100% baseline for inversion score.

sources: Kyiv Post, June 11 2026 — R-280 corridor traffic down 71% over two weeks (Brovdi). Primary GLOC linking Russia to Crimea via Mariupol/Berdiansk/Melitopol.
Kyiv Independent, June 10 2026 — Mariupol port disabled: substations, radar, fuel storage, and control tower struck by Ukrainian forces (Azov Corps). Port assessed no longer operational.
ISW: cascading GLOC effects on Crimea resupply likely.
RFE/RL June 18 2026 — Moscow Kapotnya oil refinery struck for second time in one week; fuel rationing confirmed in 53 Russian regions.
Energy Intelligence via Inkorr June 18 2026 — 16 refinery facilities hit total, including 8 of Russia's 10 largest; ~2.14 million barrels/day offline; crude processing at 21-year low.

ISW Jun 20 2026 — gas storage (Hlibivske), thermal power plant (Tavriyska), and compression stations struck; new target categories beyond prior refinery/bridge campaign. Moscow Kapotnya refinery confirmed suspended indefinitely.
Kyiv Post Jun 19 2026 — sustained escalation confirmed across new infrastructure categories.
monthly_readings.strike_score_methodology: Mariupol port strike June 10: export_terminal (3pts) + storage (2pts) + control infrastructure (3pts structural-outage classification) = 8pts. Moscow Kapotnya June 12 + June 18 (two hits): refinery (3pts × 2) = 6pts additional. 16 facilities total including 8 of Russia's 10 largest — cumulative series now at 95/100, reflecting ~2.14 million bbl/day offline.
monthly_readings.export_pressure_methodology: R-280 corridor -71% in two weeks. Inverted: 100 - (29% remaining traffic as % of baseline) = 71, then adjusted to 77 for partial-month effect and Mariupol port compound loss. Held at 77 pending updated corridor report.
monthly_readings.note: Jun 21 update: RPI 92→96. Sustained escalation: gas storage (Hlibivske), thermal power plant (Tavriyska), and compression stations now struck alongside refinery/bridge campaign — new target categories, not just repeated coverage of prior strikes. Moscow Kapotnya refinery confirmed suspended indefinitely. Capped at 96, not full proposed range, to preserve headroom above what's needed for a genuinely categorical event (e.g. confirmed Black Sea export halt, refining capacity <50%). Sources: ISW Jun 20, Kyiv Post Jun 19. | Jun 23–24: Sevastopol main substation destroyed (full city blackout). Simferopol TPP struck. Gazprom Pererabotka Orenburg plant struck (60% of GPP gas processing capacity, 1,200km from front). Russia's only helium plant hit. Kerch Bridge closed 5+ hours. Front-line fuel shortages operationally confirmed: ISW reports Hulyaipole sector troops walking 50km on foot, reconnaissance drone activity down two-thirds. Sources: Antikor/UNN Jun 24, ISW Jun 23. | Jun 25–27: NORSI refinery (Russia's 4th largest, Kstovo/Nizhny Novgorod) halted after CDU-5 unit strike — 15M ton/year capacity offline. Ufa dual-refinery strikes (Bashneft-Novoil + Bashneft-Ufaneftekhim) — combined 10.5M tons annual capacity. Russia negotiating emergency gasoline imports from Kazakhstan (~50,000 MT) and drafting tax subsidies for India imports — unprecedented. Diesel export ban under consideration (Novak, Jun 26). Fuel rationing now confirmed in at least 56 regions (up from 53). Moscow Oil Refinery offline for minimum 6 months. Paradox: damaged refineries redirecting unprocessed crude to export — Baltic/Black Sea crude loadings on track for record ~2.7M bpd June. Sources: Reuters/Ukrainska Pravda Jun 25, Mezha.net Jun 26, ISW Jun 26, EA WorldView Jun 25. | Jun 28-30: Slavyansk-na-Kubani refinery struck overnight Jun 28 (Krasnodar Krai) — ~4M ton/year capacity, named key Crimea fuel supplier, fire ongoing Jun 29. Yaroslavl refinery struck same night. Novorossiysk area drone activity overnight Jun 29-30, explosions near seaport. Russian gasoline production confirmed ~25% below year-on-year; 25-33% of total refining capacity offline. Trucking tariffs on M-14 Crimea highway up minimum 10% from Jul 1; cargo transport costs 260,000-500,000 roubles per load. Sources: ABC News/AP Jun 28, Kyiv Independent Jun 29, Censor.NET Jun 30. | Jul 1–3: Ukrainian MoD discloses June DeepStrike campaign: 11 refineries, 7 fuel logistics facilities, 8 defense-industrial enterprises, max range 2,000+km. Russia crude processing 3.95M bpd in June — 25% YoY decline, lowest in over 2 decades (AP Jul 1). Bloomberg: seaborne crude exports record 4.13M bpd but gross export revenues $1.9B/week (lowest since March 2026) — volume cannot compensate for price. NORSI Kstovo struck second time Jul 2 (AVT-6 unit, 8 days after CDU-5 strike). Donetsk–Mariupol highway bridge near Kremenivka destroyed Jul 1 (multiple spans, +15–20km detour). USF 48-hour operation Jul 1–2 disables 13 energy targets across Crimea/Luhansk/Donetsk. Russia importing refined gasoline from India for first time (400,000 MT/month planned). Sources: Ukrainian MoD Jul 1, Bloomberg/ISW Jul 1, Euromaidan Press/ISW Jul 2, Kyiv Independent Jul 2. | Jul 3–9: RPI reaches index ceiling (100). Omsk Gazprom Neft refinery struck Jul 6 — Russia's largest, 12% of national refining output, 2,500km from border, deepest strike of war; ELOU-AVT-11 primary refining unit on fire. Ukraine has now struck all 11 largest Russian gasoline producers (Ukrainian MoD confirmed). ISW Jul 6: 42.7% of total Russian projected refining capacity now disabled. Russia imposes diesel export ban effective Jul 8 through Jul 31 — national policy response confirming domestic tools exhausted (AFP/Reuters Jul 8). Novorossiysk — Russia's primary Black Sea oil terminal — zero gasoline at all public stations Jul 3 (Meduza Jul 3). Azov tanker interdiction campaign: 19 fuel tankers struck over 72 hours as Russia pivoted Crimea resupply to seaborne routes after M-14/R-280 disruption. TAIF-NK Tatarstan refinery and Saratov refinery struck Jul 7–8. St. Petersburg oil terminal struck Jul 4. Slavneft-Yanos Yaroslavl and Novatek Ust-Luga struck Jul 6. Russia importing jet fuel from Japan via multi-leg intermediary routing; emergency gasoline from India (400,000 MT/month planned) and Kazakhstan. Crude processing 3.91M bpd June — 20+ year low. RPI at ceiling: new events captured in notes and key_events; dial cannot differentiate critical from catastrophic. Sources: ISW Jul 6, AFP/Reuters Jul 8, Meduza Jul 3, ISW Jul 7. | Jul 10–17: RPI holds at 100 (ceiling). Maritime blockade of Crimea now complete — structural completion, not incremental pressure. 147 vessels struck Jul 6–16 (USF Jul 17). Sea of Azov vessel count: 132 → 43 (55% AIS decline, ISW Jul 13). Don-Azov Canal navigation halted Jul 10–11 (Reuters). Kerch Strait passage requests suspended. Kerch Bridge closed 11+ hours overnight Jul 12–13. Russia pivoted to seaborne resupply after highway/bridge degradation — that route is now also closed. Crimea isolated on all supply vectors simultaneously: land corridors degraded, maritime blockade complete, Kerch Bridge periodic closure. Salavat Gazpromneftekhim complex struck Jul 13–14 — last major Russian gasoline producer not previously hit in 2026 (ISW Jul 14). Afipsky refinery (6.25M ton/year, Krasnodar Krai) struck Jul 14. Russian oil refining at lowest level since 2005. Russia’s gasoline production running at ~65% of average seasonal consumption. State procurement system collapsing: ten canceled fuel tenders, ambulances and utility services on reserves. Notes carry analytical weight at ceiling.

alert_thresholds.notes: Above 50 = meaningful fiscal pressure on occupation funding. Above 70 = acute threat to SCI sustainability.
